verification_metadata.methodology: Cross-referenced existing research files with web search results and academic sources

source.institution: Peking University (北京大学), School of Electronics, Carbon-based Electronics Research Center
source.researchers: Peng Lian-Mou (彭练矛) and Zhang Zhi-Yong (张志勇) team
source.citation: 2024年7月，北京大学基于碳纳米管晶体管新型器件技术，结合高效的脉动阵列架构设计，成功制备了世界首个碳纳米管基的张量处理器芯片

details.architecture: Systolic array for parallel 2-bit integer multiply-accumulate operations
details.capability: Convolution and matrix multiplication for CNNs
details.significance: First CNT-based tensor processor chip in the world

claims.relevance_notes: Important for future technology roadmap, but current CNT manufacturing is not mature for commercial edge inference chips. 2-bit operations align with ternary inference concepts.
claims.recommendation: KEEP - Cite as future technology trend, not immediate implementation option

source.citation: 北大提出首个复数大模型，2比特量化，推理仅加法，可手机部署！我们推出首个面向复数值大语言模型的2比特量化框架Fairy ±i

claims.relevance_notes: Directly relevant to Mask-Locked project. Addition-only inference dramatically simplifies hardware design. Complex-valued approach could reduce hardware complexity for ternary operations.
claims.recommendation: KEEP - This is highly relevant research that should be evaluated in Gate 0 research phase

claims.relevance_notes: Neuromorphic computing is different from ternary inference approach. Not directly applicable.
claims.recommendation: OPTIONAL - Include as competitive landscape context only

source.organization: NEDO (New Energy and Industrial Technology Development Organization)
source.project_name: 高効率・高速処理を可能とする AI チップ・次世代コンピューティングの技術開発事業
source.translation: AI Chip and Next-Generation Computing Technology Development Project for High-Efficiency and High-Speed Processing

details.scope: National-level research initiative for AI chip development
details.focus: High-efficiency, high-speed processing for AI applications
details.funding: Government-backed (NEDO is Japan's largest public R&D management organization)

claims.relevance_notes: Important to monitor competitive landscape, but NEDO projects typically target data center and industrial applications, not edge inference.

additional_research.focus: AI inference processors, 'Made in Japan' AI chips

claims.relevance_notes: MN-Core targets data center and HPC applications. Different market segment from edge inference. Monitor for potential edge derivatives.
claims.recommendation: KEEP - Cite as Japanese competitive landscape, clarify different market focus

source.project_name: 次世代エッジAI半導体研究開発事業

details.focus: Local LLM support for edge AI semiconductors

claims.relevance_notes: Directly relevant - Japan is investing in edge AI semiconductor development specifically for local LLM inference.
claims.recommendation: KEEP - Add as emerging competitive threat

source.institution: KAIST (Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology)
source.paper_title: Dual-mode 2T1C DRAM Process-In-Memory Architecture for Boolean and MAC Operations

details.capability: Dual-mode operation: Boolean logic AND Multiply-Accumulate (MAC)
details.ternary_support: Supports ternary weight operations in memory array

additional_kaist_research.paper: A Logic-Compatible eDRAM Compute-In-Memory with Embedded ADCs for Processing Neural Networks

process_compatibility.notes: 2T1C eDRAM can be implemented in standard CMOS logic processes, enabling integration with digital logic without specialized DRAM process

commercial_timeline.notes: Currently academic research. No commercial products announced. Timeline for commercial availability: 3-5 years estimated
commercial_timeline.award: Best Poster Award at ISOCC 2025 Chip Design Contest (Jae-Hyeon received award)

claims.relevance_notes: Directly applicable to Mask-Locked project. 2T1C DRAM could enable ADC-free ternary MAC operations, dramatically simplifying chip design. Logic-process compatibility is critical for integration.
claims.recommendation: KEEP - Must evaluate in Gate 0 research phase. Contact KAIST HPIC Lab for collaboration potential.

source.context: Discusses memristors and other electronic components for neural-like systems

additional_sources.title: Methods for reducing computational complexity of DNN inference
additional_sources.relevance: General DNN optimization, not specific to memristors

additional_sources.title: Modern problems of intelligent systems
additional_sources.relevance: General AI systems, not specific hardware

details.research_status: Academic research exists in Russia on memristor-based neural networks
details.applications: General neural network implementations, not specifically ternary inference

claims.relevance_notes: Russian memristor research is academic in nature. No commercial development or specific application to ternary inference found. The research appears to be theoretical rather than applied to edge AI inference chips.
claims.recommendation: MODIFY - Downgrade relevance. Remove specific 'ternary inference' association. Keep only as general memristor research context.

summary.notes: Research exists but relevance to ternary inference is overstated in v12.0

scope_assessment.notes: Fraunhofer neuromorphic research focuses on spiking neural networks and brain-inspired architectures, which differ from ternary weight inference approach used in Mask-Locked project
scope_assessment.gap: No specific research found on ternary encoding or 1.58-bit quantization hardware

claims.relevance_notes: German neuromorphic research exists but is in a different domain (spiking neural networks, brain-inspired computing) from ternary inference. Not directly applicable to Mask-Locked project architecture.
claims.recommendation: MODIFY - Clarify that Fraunhofer research is in neuromorphic/spiking domain, not ternary inference. Keep only as general European research monitoring.

summary.notes: Research exists but domain relevance is overstated in v12.0
